violations: validate_code(mode=references) FALSE POSITIVE: unknown-label warning for @SYS1996, a legacy SYS label that labels(action=info) resolves fine (ApplicationPlatform/SYS, en-US "Account number")

root_cause_hypothesis: Case PASSES: build 0 errors (fullBuild), xppbp 0 warnings/0 errors, golden match. Three side findings, none blocking. (1) ENV/harness gap, not a tool defect: the Contoso sandbox Descriptor listed only ApplicationFoundation/ApplicationPlatform/ApplicationSuite, so ANY X++ that declares a CustTable/CustTrans buffer failed to compile with "A reference to Dynamics.AX.<pkg> is required" - three serialised full builds were burned discovering Directory, then Ledger, then ContactPerson one at a time. No tool (prepare/search/validate_code references) warned that CustTrans resolves in the index but is not compilable from the target model. (2) VALIDATOR_GAP (non-blocking): validate_code(mode=references) reports legacy @SYSnnnn labels as unknown-label even when the label index resolves them (labels action=info returns en-US/cs/en-GB text for @SYS1996). It is emitted as a warning so it did not gate, but it trains the agent to ignore label warnings. (3) Round-trip fidelity of d365fo_file(action=create, class): the file it wrote had CRLF in the XML skeleton but bare LF inside every <![CDATA[ ]]> block (34 CRLF / 98 LF), whereas every Microsoft AxClass on disk is pure CRLF. The subsequent action=modify(replace-code) re-serialisation normalised the whole file to CRLF (132/0), i.e. the Update path is byte-faithful to the MS serializer and the Create path is not. Element structure itself was verified identical to a real MS AxClass (AxClass>Name,SourceCode>Declaration,Methods>Method>Name,Source; no invented elements, no missing ones). Also worth noting for case design: the instruction demands "strFmt with a label"; the first attempt strFmt("%1: %2", "@SYS1996", value) is idiomatic and is exactly what get_knowledge(topic="xpp-collections") shows, but xppbp flags it BPErrorLabelIsText because the FORMAT string must itself be a label. Switched to the single-placeholder label @SYS24991 ("Account number %1"). The KB example would make an agent fail BP on first try.
suggested_fix_area: eval harness: pin the Contoso sandbox Descriptor ModuleReferences (Directory, Ledger, ContactPerson at minimum) as part of isolation; validate_code references label resolver (legacy @SYSnnnn); d365fo_file create class writer (CRLF inside CDATA, to match IMetadataProvider.Update and the MS serializer); get_knowledge xpp-collections example (strFmt format string must be the label, else BPErrorLabelIsText)
